prepR4pcm 0.3.0

Add missing species to a phylogenetic tree

Source: R/reconcile_augment.R
reconcile_augment.Rd

When a reconciliation identifies species in the data that are absent from the tree, this function grafts them onto the tree using genus-level placement. Each missing species is added as sister to a congener (a species in the same genus already present in the tree).

Usage

reconcile_augment(
  x,
  tree,
  where = c("genus", "near"),
  branch_length = c("congener_median", "half_terminal", "zero"),
  seed = NULL,
  quiet = FALSE
)

Arguments

x

A reconciliation object (from reconcile_tree() or similar).

tree

An ape::phylo object. Must be the tree used in the reconciliation.

where

Character(1). Placement strategy:

  • "genus" (default): add as sister to a random congener.

  • "near": attach at the MRCA of all congeners in the tree (better when the genus has multiple representatives).

branch_length

Character(1). How to set the new terminal branch length:

  • "congener_median" (default): median terminal branch length of congeners in the tree.

  • "half_terminal": half the sister tip's terminal branch.

  • "zero": zero-length branch (creates a polytomy).

seed

Integer or NULL. Random seed for reproducibility when selecting a congener. Default NULL (no seed).

quiet

Logical. Suppress progress messages? Default FALSE.

Value

A list with:

tree

The augmented phylo object.

original

The original (unmodified) phylo object.

augmented

A tibble documenting each added species: species, genus, placed_near, branch_length, method, n_congeners.

skipped

A tibble of species that could not be placed: species, genus, reason.

meta

Provenance metadata.

Details

This is a convenience for exploratory analyses. Genus-level placement assumes that the missing species diverged similarly to its congeners, which may not hold. Always report which species were augmented and run sensitivity analyses comparing results with and without them.

Examples

# --- Example 1: genus-level placement with congener_median branch lengths ---
data(avonet_subset)
data(tree_jetz)
result <- reconcile_tree(avonet_subset, tree_jetz,
                         x_species = "Species1", authority = NULL)
#> ℹ Reconciling 919 data names vs 657 tree tips
#> ℹ Matching 919 x 657 names through 2 stages...
#> ℹ Stage 1/4: Exact matching...
#> ℹ Stage 2/4: Normalised matching (0 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 3/4: Synonym resolution (657 matched so far)...
#> ℹ Stage 4/4: Fuzzy matching (657 matched so far)...
#> ✔ Matched 657/919 data names to tree tips

aug <- reconcile_augment(result, tree_jetz, seed = 42)
#> ℹ Augmenting tree with 262 unresolved species...
#> ✔ Added 136 species to tree (126 skipped)
#> ! Skipped species had no congener in the tree. See $skipped for details.

# Compare original vs augmented tree
cat("Original tips:", ape::Ntip(tree_jetz), "\n")
#> Original tips: 657 
cat("Augmented tips:", ape::Ntip(aug$tree), "\n")
#> Augmented tips: 793 
cat("Added:", nrow(aug$augmented), "| Skipped:", nrow(aug$skipped), "\n")
#> Added: 136 | Skipped: 126 

# Inspect which species were added and where they were placed
head(aug$augmented[, c("species", "genus", "placed_near",
                       "branch_length", "n_congeners")])
#> # A tibble: 6 × 5
#>   species                  genus        placed_near    branch_length n_congeners
#>   <chr>                    <chr>        <chr>                  <dbl>       <int>
#> 1 Acanthiza cinerea        Acanthiza    Acanthiza lin…          5.04          13
#> 2 Calamanthus cautus       Calamanthus  Calamanthus f…          3.80           2
#> 3 Calamanthus montanellus  Calamanthus  Calamanthus f…          3.80           3
#> 4 Calamanthus pyrrhopygius Calamanthus  Calamanthus f…          3.80           4
#> 5 Gerygone citrina         Gerygone     Gerygone magn…          2.91          18
#> 6 Pyrrholaemus sagittatus  Pyrrholaemus Pyrrholaemus …         15.5            1

# Species skipped (no congener in tree)
head(aug$skipped)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 3
#>   species               genus      reason             
#>   <chr>                 <chr>      <chr>              
#> 1 Melloria quoyi        Melloria   No congener in tree
#> 2 Ceblepyris caesius    Ceblepyris No congener in tree
#> 3 Ceblepyris cinereus   Ceblepyris No congener in tree
#> 4 Ceblepyris cucullatus Ceblepyris No congener in tree
#> 5 Ceblepyris graueri    Ceblepyris No congener in tree
#> 6 Ceblepyris pectoralis Ceblepyris No congener in tree

# --- Example 2: MRCA placement with zero-length branches ---
aug_near <- reconcile_augment(result, tree_jetz,
                              where = "near",
                              branch_length = "zero",
                              seed = 42, quiet = TRUE)

cat("\nMRCA placement (zero branches):\n")
#> 
#> MRCA placement (zero branches):
cat("  Added:", nrow(aug_near$augmented), "\n")
#>   Added: 136 
# Compare: MRCA placement shows genus-level context
head(aug_near$augmented[, c("species", "placed_near", "method")])
#> # A tibble: 6 × 3
#>   species                  placed_near                                    method
#>   <chr>                    <chr>                                          <chr> 
#> 1 Acanthiza cinerea        MRCA of Acanthiza lineata, Acanthiza chrysorr… near/0
#> 2 Calamanthus cautus       MRCA of Calamanthus fuliginosus, Calamanthus … near/0
#> 3 Calamanthus montanellus  MRCA of Calamanthus cautus, Calamanthus fulig… near/0
#> 4 Calamanthus pyrrhopygius MRCA of Calamanthus montanellus, Calamanthus … near/0
#> 5 Gerygone citrina         MRCA of Gerygone modesta, Gerygone albofronta… near/0
#> 6 Pyrrholaemus sagittatus  Pyrrholaemus brunneus                          near/0
